description: Build ares-crawl — Ashish's internal Firecrawl equivalent. A Node.js crawler that takes a URL, follows links up to a configurable depth, converts each page to clean Markdown, and saves results to Firestore.
requirements: Use playwright for JS-rendered pages (npm install playwright if not present)
Use turndown or a simple custom function to convert HTML → clean Markdown (strip nav, footer, header, aside, script, style tags before converting)
Respect robots.txt — fetch and parse it before crawling, skip disallowed paths
Rate limiting: configurable delay between requests (default 1000ms), never hammer the target
Export: crawlSite(url, { depth, limit, delay }) — crawls site, returns array of page results
Export: scrapePage(url) — scrapes a single URL, returns { url, title, markdown, links[], timestamp }
Save each crawled page to Firestore collection 'crawl_results' using Admin SDK (service-account.json pattern)
Firestore doc fields: url, title, markdown, links (string[]), crawledAt (Timestamp), crawlId (shared across one crawl run)
Track visited URLs to avoid duplicates within a crawl run
On error (timeout, 404, block): log and continue — never crash the full crawl
successCriteria: node scripts/ares_crawl.js https://example.com --depth 1 --limit 5 crawls 5 pages and saves to Firestore
Each Firestore doc has url, title, markdown (clean, no HTML tags), links[], crawledAt
robots.txt is fetched and disallowed paths are skipped
Script does not crash on bad URLs or JS errors on target pages
crawlSite and scrapePage are exported and usable from other ARES scripts
context: Look at scripts/agent_connector.js and scripts/create-collections.js for Admin SDK and require patterns. Do not use dotenv — use service-account.json directly. The Firestore crawl_results collection may not exist yet — Admin SDK will create it on first write.
